observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the CT to
continuously deposit collections regularly and intact to avoid the accumulation of cash
in their custody, and continuously monitor the receipts and issuances of AFs.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CA to continue to comply with
the timely preparation of BRSs and the prompt adjustment of reconciling items in the
books; (b) CT to continuously monitor the status of the City’s bank accounts to avoid
incurrence of fees due to dormancy; and (c) utilize the identified dormant accounts
for their intended purposes.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CGSO Chief to (i) obtain
updated subsidiary records from the CAO, compare these with their RPCPPE, and
prioritize the completion of the PPE reconciliation to identify the details of the
discrepancy amounting to ₱206,329,413.85 between their records, and ensure
complete description of PPE items in the property records; and (iii) prioritize the
disposal of unserviceable properties and provide the CA with the duly accomplished
IIRUP, which shall serve as the basis for the derecognition of such PPE from the
books; (b) Fact-Finding Committee to finalize the investigation on the unlocated
equipment and prepare and facilitate the immediate submission of an investigation
report, copy furnished the AT, for appropriate action; (c) City’s Appraisal Committee
to determine the fair value of donated transportation equipment and furnish a copy
of the appraisal report to the CAO to facilitate their recognition in the books;
(d) Officer-in-Charge (OIC)-City Engineer (CE) to provide the CA with pertinent
documents on the demolished public market and stalls to serve as the basis for
derecognition; and (e) CA to (i) derecognize from the books the unlocated PPE upon
the resolution of the case; (ii) derecognize the carrying value of the demolished public
market and stalls upon receipt of necessary supporting documents from the OIC-CE;
(iii) recognize the donated transportation equipment in the books upon receipt of the
appraisal report, duly supported by the corresponding DoDs; (iv) coordinate with the
City Budget Office (CBO) Officer regarding appropriate reclassification of accounts
to ensure proper recognition of transactions in the books; and (v) make necessary
adjustments to recognize procured properties in accordance with the Revised Chart
of Accounts for LGUs per COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the LCE direct the City Legal Office (CLO) and the
CGSO Officers to coordinate their efforts in facilitating the transfer and registration
of TCTs under the City’s name for parcels of land acquired through donation to
ensure legal ownership and protect the City’s interests.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the (a) CGSO Chief
to coordinate with the OIC-CE to complete and submit the Inventory of all Local
Roads and Road Map, LRN Property Cards, and Report on LRN of the City;
(b) OIC-CE to coordinate with the CA and CGSO to assess and evaluate the projects
recorded under the Road Networks account and provide them with the complete and
detailed description and cost segregation of the City’s road components indicating
specific measurements of the surface type, maintenance costs, initial costs incurred or
valuation, date of completion, location, and other necessary data; (c) CA to (i) provide
full and adequate disclosures of the Road Networks account in the NFS as prescribed
in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008; and (ii) update and maintain the LRNLCs
for all roads and their components; and (d) Road Inventory Committee to attend
seminar workshop on LRAM to ensure proper understanding and compliance with
its provisions.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the CGSO Chief to (i) coordinate
with the CA to reconcile records and determine the cost of all properties eligible for
insurance coverage; (ii) prepare and maintain an updated inventory of insurable
properties to facilitate the timely application for insurance coverage with the GSIS;
(iiii) establish and implement an effective internal control system for the monitoring
and maintenance of government property records, to ensure prompt and complete
insurance coverage with the GSIS; and (iv) ensure that the RPCPPE contains
complete and accurate information including the year, brand, model, color, plate
number, chassis/engine number, and conduction sticker number, as applicable; (b)
remind the accountable personnel of their obligation to regularly monitor and
ensure the timely renewal of the LTO registrations of vehicles under their
responsibility; and (c) direct the CA to charge from the negligent City employees any
penalties for non-compliance with laws and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE strengthen measures to ensure the continuous
and timely implementation of PPAs funded by NGAs to fully achieve their intended
objectives and maximize the benefits for the target beneficiaries.

We recommended that the LCE (a) as Chairperson of the LSB, direct the LSB
Secretary to (i) submit copies of the quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports
using SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 to the DepEd Central Office and DBM
Regional Office; and (ii) ensure the timely posting of quarterly utilization reports on
the City’s FDB in accordance with the schedule prescribed in DILG MC No. 2024-
021 dated February 15, 2024; and (b) direct the OIC-CE to (i) coordinate with the
end-users before implementation of projects to prevent misunderstandings and
ensure alignment with their intended use; (ii) strictly monitor the status of projects
and conduct site inspection before, during, and after implementation to address
potential issues; and (iii) ensure that projects are implemented according to the
approved engineering plans.

We recommended that the LCE (a) maximize the utilization of the 20% DF
and implement the development projects within the targeted timeframe for the
promotion of the general welfare of the constituents of the City; (b) evaluate
thoroughly the proposed priority development projects to be funded under the 20%
DF as to their readiness for implementation or procurement to avoid delays or non-
implementation thereof; and (c) monitor closely the implementation of all
development projects to facilitate corrective action, if any, and the timely
reprogramming and reallocation of fund for optimum utilization.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-CE to ensure that all
infrastructure projects are provided with project signboards in compliance with the
prescribed guidelines under COA Circular No. 2013-004 to inform the public on
matters of public concern and that the City fully adopt the State’s policy on full
disclosure of all transactions involving public interest.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continuously comply with the rules and
regulations on the appropriation for the LDRRMF, and ensure its optimal utilization
to mitigate the loss of lives and properties in the event of natural disasters or
calamities; (b) require the CDRRMO to submit the Report on the Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF to the AT on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month for review and verification; and (c) require the CA to (i) consistently adhere to
the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 regarding the transfer
of the unexpended balance to ensure the availability of LDRRMF for the
implementation of other disaster risk reduction and management activities of the
City; (ii) record purchases of supplies and materials under the appropriate Inventory
accounts and recognize their issuances to end-users in the appropriate expense
accounts based on the SSMI duly supported by approved RIS at least monthly; and
(iii) verify the completeness of supporting documents prior to processing payment.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CA to properly record in the
books all revenues arising from market stall rentals, moving forward; and (b) CT and
the Market Supervisor to (i) issue follow-up demand letters to enforce the settlement
of the tenants’ arrearages; (ii) consider initiating legal action, if deemed necessary, to
facilitate the collection of long overdue accounts; (iii) ensure the immediate renewal
or execution of new lease contracts for all stall occupants with expired agreements,
making certain that the terms and conditions are updated and aligned with existing
market policies and prevailing rental rates; and (iv) strictly enforce the provisions of
the lease agreements, particularly those related to the payment obligations of stall
occupants, to prevent the recurrence of similar issues in the future.

We recommended that the LCE sustain and further strengthen the
implementation of effective and efficient solid waste management PPAs to ensure the
continued protection of public health, the preservation of the environment, and the
realization of cost savings for the City.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to sustain its commitment
to GAD by (a) ensuring the proper allocation of the required budget for GAD in
accordance with applicable guidelines; (b) preparing and submitting the GPB in a
timely manner; (c) ensuring the efficient and effective implementation of the
programmed GAD PPAs; and (d) submitting the GAD AR to oversight agencies
within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CT and the CA to continue
ensuring strict compliance with the provisions of BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the proper
withholding and timely remittance of taxes within the prescribed period; and (b) CA
to maintain close coordination with the BTECH Accountant to prevent the occurrence
of over-remittance of taxes.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the CT and CA to
continuously comply with regulations in the deduction and remittance of premiums
and loan amortizations including the government share within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the CT and CA to
continuously comply with the regulations on the withholding and remittance of
premiums and loan amortizations within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the CT and CA to
continuously comply with the regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly
contributions within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the LCE require the CT to continuously deposit collections regularly and intact to avoid the accumulation of cash in their custody.

We recommended that Management exert further efforts to establish the ownership of 32 parcels of land with total market value of ₱142,692,723.50 for proper recognition in the books pursuant to Paragraphs 26 and 27 of PPSAS 17.

We recommended that Management  direct the Municipal Accountant to (a)  coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in determining the recognized cost of the portion of land that is being held for lease for proper classification in the books; and (b)  draw adjusting entries to correctly recognize the asset being held for lease as Investment Property.

We recommended  that the Municipal Mayor  (a)  direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant  to establish the cost of unserviceable items which are part of a set to facilitate proper recognition and disposal; (b) i) instruct the GSO to locate the PPE items which were not accounted for during the conduct of the physical inventory; ii) if these cannot be located anymore, require the accountable officers and end-users  to request for relief of property accountability for the lost items; and  (c) direct  the GSO and  the Municipal  Accountant to derecognize the carrying amounts of the unserviceable properties in the Accounting and Property Records.

We recommended that Management  assign additional personnel in the General Services Office to  facilitate management, accounting and monitoring of all Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the Municipality which includes  the taking of physical count of property, plant and equipment  which shall be made annually and reported on the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE).

We recommended that Management (a) advise the Local Finance Committee and other officials concerned to regularly monitor the Municipality’s cash position; (b) ensure that cash allocated for trust liabilities are used solely for their intended purpose; (b) incur expenditures only for projects, programs, and activities that are highly necessary and cannot be dispensed with; and (d) undertake revenue-enhancing strategies and cost-cutting measures to eliminate the cash deficit.
We recommended that Management (a) carefully review and examine its revenue estimates based on collectability and (b) exert more efforts to achieve or even exceed the targeted goals.

We recommended that Management continue to satisfactorily implement the projects, programs and activities funded by National Government Agencies (NGAs) and promptly return the unutilized balances to its respective sources.
We recommended that Management continue to abide strictly with the guidelines on the planning and implementation of the projects/activities funded under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that Management (a) continue to be compliant with Section 21 of R. A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1 in the allocation of LDRRMF and institutionalizing the disaster risk reduction and management plan; (b) provide for DRRM facilities that meet the minimum standards on service delivery during calamities; and (c) ensure that evacuation centers do not expose calamity victims to further health risks.

We recommended that Management (a) enforce strictly the sanctions on violators of the ordinances/laws/regulations pertaining to solid waste management; (b) further intensify and involve the barangay governments in monitoring the compliance of its constituents with environmental laws, rules and ordinances;  (c) provide resources for the establishment of Material Recovery Facilities which would reduce the residual waste being hauled and collected by the service provider; and (d) continue to properly inform and encourage the constituents on the proper segregation of waste.

We recommended that Management continue to be compliant with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 on the withholding of the appropriate taxes from its payment of transactions and ensure remittance thereof within the prescribed period.  Remit all other collections held in trust due to Other National Government Agencies and Government Owned Controlled Corporations within the prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of each month.

We recommended that Management require the GFPS to cause the creation of the GAD database which will serve as basis for planning, programming and  formulating GAD responsive projects, programs and activities.

To ensure the reliability of balances of PPE accounts at yearend, we reiterated our recommendation that Management (a) ensure the complete conduct of physical inventory of all property owned by the Municipality and the reconciliation of property records maintained by the GSO and the Accounting Office; (b) instruct the municipal officials concerned to continue exerting efforts in the retrieval and reproduction of land titles and other documents required to establish ownership and cost valuation of unrecorded land and buildings to facilitate its recognition in the books; (c) initiate procedures for the proper disposal and recording of obsolete, unserviceable and fully depreciated items in the PPE; and (d) consider requesting authority from COA to write off unidentified or unsupported beginning balances of PPE accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 93-404.
